application.definition: A request to send your camper to camp. Each camper needs their own application for each camp session.

applicant.definition: A parent or guardian who is filling out or has submitted a camp application on behalf of a camper.

camper.definition: The child who will be attending camp. Each camper has their own record and can have separate applications for different sessions.

draft.definition: An application you have started but not yet submitted. Drafts are private — camp staff cannot see them until you submit.

submitted.definition: An application you have finished and sent to camp staff for review. Once submitted, you cannot edit the application directly.

under_review.definition: A camp staff member has opened your application and is actively reviewing it. No action is needed from you unless staff reach out.

approved.definition: Your application was accepted and your camper has a confirmed spot. Check your inbox for next steps from camp staff.

rejected.definition: Your application was not accepted. The reason is usually shown in the application details. You may be able to apply again for a different session.

waitlisted.definition: Your application is on hold because there are no open spots right now. If another applicant withdraws, you may be offered a spot and will be notified by inbox message.

withdrawn.definition: You cancelled your own application. A withdrawn application cannot be un-withdrawn — you would need to start a new application.

required_document.definition: A document that every applicant must provide. Required documents include an immunization record, insurance card, and medical exam form. Your application cannot be fully approved without them.

requested_document.definition: A document that a camp staff member has specifically asked you to provide. Requested documents appear as cards on your Documents page with instructions from the staff member.

mfa.definition: An extra login step that uses a code from your phone in addition to your password. MFA makes your account much harder to break into, even if your password is stolen.

medical_provider.definition: A camp nurse, doctor, or medical staff member who has access to camper medical records and treatment logs through the Medical portal.

paper_application.definition: An application submitted by uploading scanned paper forms instead of filling out the online form. Paper applications go through the same review process as digital ones.

digital_application.definition: An application filled out directly in the online form. All sections are completed in the browser and submitted electronically.

hipaa.definition: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — a federal law that protects the privacy of medical information. The system follows HIPAA rules and logs all access to medical records.

admin.definition: A camp staff member who reviews applications, manages camper records, sends messages to families, and oversees the application workflow.

super_admin.definition: An admin with additional privileges, including managing user accounts, viewing the system audit log, and adjusting access permissions.

session.definition: A specific week or period of camp — for example, Week 3 (June 14-21). Each application is tied to a particular session. You can apply for more than one session.

inbox.definition: The messaging center inside the portal where all communication between applicants and camp staff takes place. Messages here create a permanent record.

archived.definition: A message thread or session that has been put aside but not deleted. Archived items can be found in the Archived folder and restored at any time.

phi.definition: Any information that identifies a person and relates to their health, medical conditions, or treatment. PHI is protected by HIPAA and must never be shared outside the system.
